title: Preparation and Point Counting in Sharply O-minimal Structures
speaker: Oded Carmon
abstract:
I will describe recent work in progress on logarithmic--exponential preparation theorems in analytically generated sharply o-minimal structures. Our results imply the sharp o-minimality of ℝexp
 as well as a uniform version of Wilkie’s conjecture on the density of rational points in definable sets. Our approach is based on the preparation theorem of Lion--Rolin and its extension to the o-minimal setting by van den Dries--Speissegger, and on the theory of complex cells introduced by Binyamini--Novikov.  
This is joint work with Gal Binyamini and Dmitry Novikov.

proof_stages.label: Point-counting goal and sharp complexity formalism
proof_stages.summary: The speaker recalls Pila-Wilkie counting outside the algebraic part, Wilkie's polylogarithmic conjecture for R_exp, and introduces format-degree filtrations and sharp o-minimality as the quantitative language needed to control definable complexity.

proof_stages.label: Analytic generation replacing sharp derivatives
proof_stages.summary: After reviewing earlier restricted sharp point-counting results based on sharp derivatives, the talk replaces that hypothesis by analytic generation, where definable functions have controlled holomorphic continuations to complex cells and hence support effective Yomdin-Gromov type estimates.

proof_stages.label: Effective S-preparation on complex cells
proof_stages.summary: For functions definable in the analytically generated base structure, the domain is effectively covered by cells on which each function is a bounded unit times a shifted monomial, with exponents and units controlled via winding numbers, intersections, and hyperbolic-metric estimates.

proof_stages.label: LE-preparation after adjoining unrestricted exponentiation
proof_stages.summary: Because unrestricted exp introduces new asymptotic growth types, the preparation theorem is upgraded to log-exp scales: on pieces, an S_exp-definable function is expressed using logarithmic scales, exponential factors, units, and a lower-dimensional coefficient, so its sign is reduced to fewer variables.

proof_stages.label: Effective quantifier elimination and sharpness of the exponential expansion
proof_stages.summary: The lower-dimensional sign reduction from LE-preparation yields effective quantifier elimination, which in turn defines the correct format-degree filtration by prepared pieces and proves sharpness for suitable exponential expansions, including the route to sharpness of R_exp.

proof_stages.label: Algebraic approximation and induction for polylogarithmic point counting
proof_stages.summary: Using per-coordinate log-exp scales and holomorphic cells, definable sets are approximated by same-dimensional semi-algebraic sets of controlled degree; polynomial interpolation and dimension induction then give uniform polylogarithmic bounds for rational and algebraic points, including Wilkie's conjecture for R_exp.
